name: Content / media / publishing platform
summary: A read-heavy platform that ingests, manages, and delivers content (articles, images, audio, video) to a large audience, spanning editorial or user-generated content lifecycles, media processing, and CDN-scale distribution. Read scale, delivery performance, content governance, and rights dominate.

concerns: very high read-to-write ratio; multi-tier caching (CDN edge, origin shield, application cache) and cache-invalidation correctness
cache-key/variant strategy (device, geo, personalization, A/B) without cache fragmentation or stale leaks
traffic spikes from virality, breaking news, or scheduled releases; origin protection and stampede/dogpile control
media delivery: adaptive-bitrate streaming (HLS/DASH), startup time, rebuffer ratio, CDN egress cost
image/asset optimization (responsive sizes, modern formats, lazy loading) and Core Web Vitals
questions: Is the CDN/multi-tier caching and invalidation strategy specified, including variant/cache-key design?
Is behaviour under viral/breaking-news spikes defined (origin shielding, stampede control)?
For media, are streaming QoE targets (startup, rebuffer ratio) and egress-cost limits stated?

concerns: content model: structured content types, fields, taxonomies, tags, and relationships (headless/API-first)
versioning, drafts, scheduled publish/unpublish, and content history/rollback
media asset management: originals, renditions/derivatives, metadata, and storage tiering
localization/translation of content variants and fallbacks
search/recommendation index consistency with published content; denormalized read models
structured/SEO metadata (schema.org, Open Graph, sitemaps, canonical URLs)
questions: Is the structured content model, taxonomy, versioning, and scheduled publish/unpublish specified?
Is media asset management (originals, renditions, metadata, tiering) defined?
Are search-index consistency and SEO/structured-metadata requirements stated?

concerns: editorial workflow: authoring, review, approval, embargo, and multi-role publishing pipeline
content moderation for UGC: pre/post-moderation, automated classifiers, human review queues, appeals
comments/social features and abuse handling (spam, harassment, brigading)
content lifecycle: publish, update, syndicate, archive, takedown/depublish, and expiry
personalization/feeds/recommendation ranking and its editorial controls
questions: Is the editorial authoring/review/approval and embargo workflow specified?
Is the UGC moderation pipeline (automated + human review + appeals) defined?
Are content-lifecycle operations including takedown/depublish and archival specified?

concerns: publishing pipeline and cache/CDN purge orchestration on publish/update/takedown
media processing pipeline: ingest, transcode/encode farm, packaging, and failure/retry
content-delivery monitoring (cache hit ratio, CDN health, QoE) and multi-CDN failover
cost operations for storage and CDN egress at scale
scheduled/embargoed release automation and rollback of a bad publish
questions: Is cache/CDN purge orchestration on publish/update/takedown specified?
Is the media transcode pipeline with failure/retry and monitoring defined?
Is multi-CDN failover and egress-cost operation addressed?

concerns: content rights/licensing: DRM, geo-restriction/geoblocking, licensing windows and expiry, watermarking
copyright/DMCA and EU DSA notice-and-takedown, repeat-infringer, and transparency-reporting obligations
content moderation legal duties (illegal content, minors' safety, CSAM reporting, age assurance)
UGC liability, terms of service, and creator/rights-holder agreements
GDPR/CCPA consent, ad/tracking consent, and audience-data handling
questions: Are content rights (DRM, licensing windows, geoblocking) and their enforcement specified?
Are notice-and-takedown (DMCA/DSA) and illegal-content/minor-safety obligations captured?
Are consent and audience-data privacy requirements defined?

concerns: protection of premium/paywalled content: entitlement checks, signed URLs, token-based media access, DRM key handling
anti-piracy: leech/hotlink protection, session/stream-sharing limits, forensic watermarking
CMS/admin security: RBAC for editorial roles, protection against defacement and malicious publish
UGC as an attack surface: stored XSS, malicious uploads, SSRF via remote media fetch
bot/scraping abuse and content-theft protection
questions: Are paywalled/premium content entitlements enforced (signed URLs, DRM, token access)?
Is UGC upload/embedding sanitized against stored XSS, malicious files, and SSRF?
Is editorial RBAC and protection against unauthorized/malicious publish specified?

concerns: reading/viewing experience: typography, responsive layout, media playback controls, continuity/resume
content discovery: search, navigation, related content, and personalization
creator/editor authoring ergonomics (rich editor, preview, media embedding)
engagement features (save, share, follow, subscribe) and clear paywall/registration walls
questions: Is the reading/viewing experience (responsive layout, playback controls, resume) specified?
Are content-discovery and navigation expectations defined?
Is the authoring/editor experience for creators specified?

concerns: media accessibility: captions/subtitles, audio description, transcripts (and legal mandates for broadcast/public bodies)
WCAG 2.2 AA for reading and player UI; keyboard-operable custom media players
localization/i18n of content and UI, including RTL and locale formats
readable typography, contrast, and reflow for long-form content
questions: Are captions/subtitles, transcripts, and audio-description requirements specified for media?
Is the media player keyboard-operable and WCAG 2.2 AA conformant?
Are localization/i18n requirements for content and UI stated?

concerns: read availability decoupled from the authoring/publishing plane (a CMS outage must not take the site down)
graceful serving of stale content on origin failure (stale-while-revalidate)
durability of media originals and content history; backup and restore
resilience of the media pipeline to partial encode failures
questions: Is delivery availability decoupled from the publishing plane, with stale-serve on origin failure?
Are durability and backup of media originals and content history specified?

concerns: audience engagement outcomes (retention, watch/read time, return visits) and subscriber conversion
content-discovery effectiveness (users find relevant content)
trust and brand safety (moderation quality, ad adjacency)
questions: Are audience-engagement and conversion outcome metrics defined?
Are content-discovery effectiveness and brand-safety outcomes specified?
